title: Shell Oil top contributor in campaign to stop Alaska coastal initiative
summary: As Shell prepares to explore for offshore oil in Alaska's Arctic, the company is also waging an effort against a ballot initiative that supporters say would restore the voice of Alaskans in federal coastal decisions.

title: Alaska Native suicide rate more than twice that of non-Natives
summary: A new report says 65 percent of 1,347 violent deaths in Alaska between 2003 and 2008 were suicides, and that the high rate of suicide among Alaska Natives did not change significantly during the study period.

title: Money Local subsidiary lets most of its Cook Inlet leases lapse Linc Energy (Alaska) Inc., the local subsidiary of an Au…
summary: Money Local subsidiary lets most of its Cook Inlet leases lapse Linc Energy (Alaska) Inc., the local subsidiary of an Australian independent, allowed 26 state of Alaska leases to expire in early June, keeping one state lease currently proposed for unitization, its Alaska Mental Health Trust leases and exploration licenses, and its Cook Inlet regional Native corporation leases. TIM BRADNER Good oil times are ending, so what can we do? We've reached a dubious milestone in our state's history. For the first time, Alaska's revenues will turn down mainly because of the continued decline in oil production and not because of some wobble in oil prices. title: Island of reindeer ghosts may be the most remote place in Alaska
summary: Owing to the distance of St. Matthew from the regular travel routes, opportunities to visit are rare. It is barren, treeless, uninhabited -- and fascinating.

title: Bristol Palin back for another bout on 'Dancing with the Stars'
summary: Bristol Palin will join a dozen celebrities for the first all-star version of Dancing with the Stars in September on ABC. For those keeping score at home, heres your Palin Celebrity Television Scorecard.

title: Conoco's $551 million profit in Alaska down from first quarter
summary: On Wednesday, politicians were spinning ConocoPhillipss second-quarter earnings in the context of Alaska's oil tax debate.

title: Alaska Air Force station upgrade included in big US defense contract
summary: Clear Air Force Station in the Alaska Interior is set to receive a chunk of contracted cash to update its Early Warning Radar system.

title: Don Young's unusual endorsement of Hawaii Democrat Mazie Hirono
summary: Alaska Republican stalwart Don Young has appeared in a campaign ad lending support to Hawaii's Mazie Hirono, a Democrat, leading to much political hay over the House Representative's decision.

title: Canada immigration ban leads to strip club high school recruitment
summary: Candian adult entertainment lobby creates brochure to distribute at high school career fairs promoting the benefits of life on the dance pole.

title: Man appears superhuman in battle with Wasilla police
summary: A homeless man apparently known to law enforcement officers throughoutAlaska had an especially notable confrontation with Wasilla police lastweek, reports The Mat-Su Valley Frontiersman.Robert Compton was reported to be threatening toward a man and hischildren for no apparent reason, police said. When officer Rick ManriquelocatedCompton, he pulled a knife and demanded afight andeventually to be shot. Manrique called for help.

title: Is Parnell punting on Alaska health care?
summary: Gov. Sean Parnell often resists federal involvement in Alaska's affairs. And yet he just invited Obama's Health department to take control of the state's health care market. What's up with that?

title: Discovery Channel back in Alaska with 'Yukon Men'
summary: The Discovery Channel is back in Alaska, this time turning its lens towards life in an Interior village in 'Yukon Men'.

title: Retired couple catches century-old, 1,100-pound white sturgeon in B.C.
summary: Michael Snell proclaimed It is the most excitement Ive ever had with a fish after reeling in his 12-foot-long fish with a head more than 3 feet wide, and weighing 1,100 pounds, on the Fraser River in British Columbia.

title: While most of America sizzles, Alaskans shiver through chilly summer
summary: How are residents of the icebox handling this coldest-Alaskan-summer-ever talk while many in the Lower 48 sizzle in record heat?

title: EU considers border security drones as Mediterranean death toll reaches 170
summary: At least 170 refugees and economic migrants have died braving the Mediterranean Sea in makeshift floats bound for Europe from Africa. In response, Europe proposes beefing up border security with unmanned aerial drones.

title: Mount Marathon runner who vanished fading into background
summary: Now only time will tell whether Michael LeMaitre is found or joins the many gone lost forever in the 49th state.

title: 5 graves unearthed during construction in Juneau
summary: In June, construction on school grounds unearthed a headstone and body; now the city has unearthed five individuals on the site.

title: Damascus bombing: Too early to celebrate, Syrian activists say
summary: Damascus bombing that killed top Syrian officials met with joy and skepticism among Syrian activists.

title: One-time Senate candidate Joe Miller reinvents himself as online news publisher
summary: A publisher with a politician's heart: Failed 2010 U.S. Senate candidate Joe Miller is now a bona fide media publisher. What's his ultimate goal?

title: Rising water threatens El Salvador coastline
summary: Salvadoran villagers say rising water is destroying their coast.

title: Governor: Federal government on hook for Alaska health insurance exchange
summary: Exchanges are meant to offer a one-stop shop to find health insurance options, prices and features -- and then compare that information easily. Alaska Gov. Sean Parnell said Tuesday that it wasn't in the state's interests to create and finance its own exchange.

title: Will Sarah Palin speak at GOP convention?
summary: Sarah Palin still hasn't received an invitation to speak at the 2012 Republican National Convention in Tampa, Fla., and tea partiers aren't happy.

title: DEA brings deadly force to Honduras
summary: Will Washingtons Operation Anvil drive drug traffickers elsewhere?

title: Assessor: Baptist Temple owes back taxes
summary: The Anchorage Baptist Temple improperly claimed tax-free status for the homes of two of its ministers and now owes the city more than $61,000 in back taxes, the Anchorage Assessor has ruled.

title: It is the coldest July on record, so far
summary: It's not in your head: The National Weather Service says that so far this is the coldest July on record in Anchorage. The average temperature for the first 12 days of this month -- typically the warmest of the year -- was just 52.7 degrees.

title: Moving Alaska glacier complicates recovery efforts at Air Force plane crash site
summary: See photos from the site of a 1952 Air Force plane crash that killed 52 airmen. Military crews are still recovering debris and possibly human remains - but progress is dictated by a belching glacier.

title: Alaska float plane crash kills former state lawmaker
summary: Former Anchorage Rep. Cheryll Heinze died Tuesday night in a float plane crash on Beluga Lake near Homer on Kenai Peninsula, Alaska State Troopers reported.

title: Sailor dodges death on solo Gulf of Alaska trip
summary: Sixty-year-old Russian Rimas Meleshyus dreamed of a sailing trip around the world, yet after his venture across the Gulf of Alaska, he is lucky to be alive.

title: Anchorage House candidate ruled ineligible over residency
summary: A Republican House candidate who claims to have been homeless and living in her pickup camper while dodging the cops in Muldoon has been ruled ineligible for office on residency grounds.

title: USA: Mermaids aren't real
summary: NOAA has issued a statement proclaiming that no evidence has ever been found of the existence of the half-human, half-fish women of mythology.

title: Alaska Senate looks for public input on high gas prices
summary: The Alaska Senate working group on energy will hold a meeting in Anchorage Wednesday, and are asking for the public's input on high gas and heating oil prices in the state.

title: Polynesian community reassured after meeting with Anchorage police
summary: Anchorage Police Chief Mark Mew addressed community concerns following fatal police shootings and a scheduled rally asking for answers.

title: Polynesian community questions police policies after shooting deaths
summary: A new nonprofit is reaching out to Anchorage police, looking for answers after two shooting deaths by APD last month.

title: Alaska legislator vows to get to bottom of high gas prices
summary: Petersen's legislation, like that of Sen. Bill Wielechowski, who is sponsoring similar legislation in the Senate, would regulate refiners like other utilities.

title: Dramatic rescue saves teenager trapped in glacier crevasse
summary: The Valdez Fire Department last weekend rescued a teen tourist from Connecticut who fell into a crevasse on Worthington Glacier. Much of the boy's clothing was ripped off, leaving him with skin on ice for nearly five hours.

title: Adult circumcision push fails in AIDS prevention campaign
summary: US effort doesn't go over well in Swaziland, the country with the highest HIV infection rate in the world.

title: 'Leap second' wreaks havoc for major websites, world clocks
summary: Extra second added to end of day June 30 -- so-called leap second -- causes major websites to crash, flight delays, and lots of peeved time keepers.

title: Health care for thousands at stake in Alaska governor's Medicaid decision
summary: Under the federal health reform law, states stand to receive billions of dollars to expand Medicaid to lower-income residents. Will Gov. Sean Parnell opt out of Medicaid expansion?
